name: Brenda's Black Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Vinegar (if using a sweet orange or tangerine)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the beans in a soup pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil, then turn off the heat.
2. Let the beans cool completely.
3. In a separate pan, cook the onions and bell peppers in half the oleo until they are limp.
4. Add the cooked onions and bell peppers, along with the ham hocks and orange (or tangerine), to the cooled beans. Cover generously with water.
5. Bring the mixture back to a simmer, then turn off the heat.
6. Stir in the flour mixed with a bit of oleo until well combined.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
8. If the soup is too thick, add more water to reach the desired consistency.
9. Serve hot with cornbread and sliced tomatoes.
This recipe is transcribed exactly as it appears in "White Trash Cooking" by Ernest Matthew Mickler (1986).
